evidence_quotes.quote: always online component (so they can implement and exploit predatory lootboxes or any other gatcha gizmos) into the games
life time updates are removed from the EULA and your bought software stops getting any major updates.
Target even software publishers in a way so they cant alter (in destructive scamy way) or destroy their product whenever they like

paragraphs.text: Hello, i would suggest to slightly widen the scope of the Fairness Act. Target even software publishers in a way so they cant alter (in destructive scamy way) or destroy their product whenever they like and. Usualy it works like this, you buy a software with extra charge that promisses you life time updates. Few years down the line, life time updates are removed from the EULA and your bought software stops getting any major updates. (couch couch Filmora). Thats the "good" ending, there are worse, in the gaming industry. It starts the same like the Filmora above, you buy some game you like, even its ultimated (more expensive edition), may it be digital or physical boxed copy. After few years this games publisher decides to kill the game completly. By adding always online component (so they can implement and exploit predatory lootboxes or any other gatcha gizmos) into the games, they need to keep some server infrastructure on their end, which generates some cost, and when the game doesnt generate enough or they want to force their players to migrate to new version......they just pull the plug. thats the "bad" ending (game the Crew). how to solve this? well in my simple eyes, there are two ways, refund your customers when you cancel their access with the full price they paid. the other less pricy would be to force publishers to leave the software/game in the usable state, for example provide files so ppl can host their own servers for that online component. I am 40 ish internet user and i still remember there were servers you could host in you garage or at profesional server providers around the globe. There are still quake and battlefield servers around :). I just dont want to lose what i bought to own forever, i wouldnt buy such thing if they would tell us "we are gonna shutdown this game when we release the next one" thank you for reading this far Milan
